test: hadron variable-power draw follows the recipe, not the machine
summary: The probe under-plumbs the coal feed (tier-3 belt = 270/min) while a full-clock Hadron on Recipe_Diamond_C needs 600 coal/min, so the solver honestly underclocks the machine to 45% and reports 174 MW; the code and the "draw follows the recipe" expectation are both correct.
detail: Reproduced the exact failure (totalPowerMw=173.9970624457669) via a Session::in_memory repro of the probe's first half, then confirmed the fix. The gamedata layer resolves Recipe_Diamond_C.variable_power_mw = Some(500.0) correctly (crates/gamedata/src/db.rs:168 recipe_power; verified live and by existing unit test crates/gamedata/src/docs.rs:1174). The solver power law crates/solver/src/t1.rs:375 is power_mw*count*clock^1.321928 and follows the recipe. The failure is that the derived Diamond out-rate is 13.5/min, not 30 — the machine is legitimately starved to clock 0.45, and 500*0.45^1.321928 = 173.998, matching exactly. Cause: a full-clock Hadron on Recipe_Diamond_C (20 Coal -> 1 Diamond @ 2s) draws 600 Coal/min, but the probe wires the coal edge at tier:3 (renderer/e2e-audit/gamedata-probes.spec.ts:271). BELT_CAPACITY (crates/planner-core/src/entities.rs:180 = [60,120,270,480,780,1200]) makes tier 3 a Mk.3 belt capped at 270/min; 270/20 = 13.5 Diamond/min is the binding constraint. The probe's comment "Ample (uncapped) coal supply" conflates the uncapped PORT ceiling (rateCeiling:null) with the EDGE belt tier, which still caps flow. Corrected expectation: the feature-level claim (variable-power draw = 500 MW / 1000 MW at full clock) is right and the code honors it — re-running with the coal edge at tier 5 (780/min >= 600) yields totalPowerMw=500 and outRate=30. This is a probe-wiring defect, not a code bug, and matches no pass-1 finding. (The DarkMatter half at line 317 would pass — its 30/min diamond feed through a tier-3 belt is not binding — but the test fails first at line 284.)
fixHint: In renderer/e2e-audit/gamedata-probes.spec.ts, raise the coal edge tier on the Recipe_Diamond_C wiring (line 271, eCoal add_edge) from 3 to 5 (or 6) so the Mk.3 belt's 270/min cap doesn't starve the ~600 coal/min a full-clock Hadron needs; leave the diamond/darkmatter edges at tier 3 (those flows are 30/min, well under 270/min).

test: import synthesizes a save-only node claim + override off the catalog
summary: The probe's declared expectation is wrong: it places the miner 1.27M m from the only machine, so extractor→cluster attribution (a 360 m radius) drops it before bind_extractors' save-only synthesis can run — the code is correct.
detail:
Failure: expect(claims).toHaveLength(1) fails with 0 claims (import-probes.spec.ts:311). facs.length===1 (line 306) passes, so the lone smelter formed its one cluster/factory; the miner simply never became a claim.

Root cause (all in crates/app/src/import.rs): the claim-synthesis loop at import.rs:691 iterates ONLY c.extractors — extractors already attributed to that cluster. Attribution is done in cluster()'s second pass, import.rs:416-440: each extractor is attached to its nearest cluster centroid ONLY IF within DBSCAN_EPS_M * 3.0 = 360 m (the filter at import.rs:425; DBSCAN_EPS_M = 120, import.rs:131). The probe (import-probes.spec.ts:287-302) puts the sole smelter at (0,0,0) — centroid (0,0) — and the miner at (900000,900000), which is ≈1,272,792 m from the centroid. The 360 m filter rejects it → nearest = None → miner dropped → c.extractors empty → bind_extractors (import.rs:170) and the claim loop (import.rs:691-707) never execute → zero claims and zero overrides.

The probe header conflates two unrelated thresholds: NODE_MATCH_M = 250 m (bind_extractors' catalog-node match that selects the save-only synthesis branch at import.rs:234-253) versus the 360 m extractor-to-cluster attribution radius. The author moved the miner to (900000,900000) to make it "off the catalog" (>250 m from every bundled node), not realizing that placement also puts it 1.27M m from the only machine, which is what actually kills the test. The bind_extractors save-only path the probe claims to pin is never reached.

Not a code bug: a real Satisfactory miner is belt-connected to the factory it feeds and is therefore always co-located with it; a miner 1.27M m from every machine is a physically impossible save, and the pipeline correctly discards an orphaned extractor with no nearby cluster. Corrected expectation: to exercise bind_extractors' save-only synthesis, the extractor must be attributed (within 360 m of the machine cluster) AND off-catalog (>250 m from every bundled node). The clean way is to co-locate the smelter and the miner far off the catalog together — then the cluster centroid sits by the miner, the miner is attributed, and it is beyond NODE_MATCH_M of every catalog node, so the code synthesizes exactly one save:BP_ResourceNode_TEST1 claim + override as the header describes.
fixHint: Fix the probe, not the code. Co-locate the smelter and miner off-catalog: set the smelter to x:900000,y:900000,z:0 AND the miner to x:900000,y:900050 (≈50 m apart, well within the 360 m attribution radius, still >250 m from every bundled catalog node). Then keep the assertions as-is (node == "save:BP_ResourceNode_TEST1", saveNodeId == "BP_ResourceNode_TEST1", one override with pos≈900000,900050). Alternatively place the miner within 360 m of the origin smelter but >250 m from all catalog nodes; the co-locate-off-catalog variant is simpler and guaranteed.

test: reuse scales up the rod machine count when capacity is short
summary: The probe's expectation is correct and the product code deterministically produces exactly it (rod count 2 / clock 1.0 / one rod group, screw count 2, port-out 80); the single failure was a flake, not a code defect or a wrong expectation.
detail: Traced the full scale-up path in renderer/src/graph/MakeFromResources.tsx build() (lines 380-439) against the seeded scenario (ingot IN ceiling 200; one Recipe_IronRod_C machine at clock 1.0 = 15/min with NO downstream consumer; MAKE 80 Iron Screw with reuse ON = 20/min rod demand). The reused rod line has no consumer and no world OUT port, so under the demand-driven solve its derived output is 0: t1.rs minimizes Sigma machines with elastic output targets (t1.rs:130-172), and t0.rs yields machines_exact~=0 -> out_rates 0 (t0.rs:463-485). Session derive calls solver::t1::solve with T0Edit::Recompute (session.rs:2000,2501), confirming committed=0 at MakeFromResources.tsx:423. Then newDemand=20 (lines 385-387), capacityNow=15*1*1=15 (line 424), needed=0-0+20=20 (line 430), 20>15 -> needCount=ceil(20/(15*1))=2 (line 432), emitting one set_group_count{rod,2} (line 438). SetGroupCount sets g.count and leaves g.clock untouched for a non-Built group (planner-core/src/commands.rs:963-965), so rod stays clock 1.0. Rod remains a leaf in buildCp (it is in effectiveAvailable) so no duplicate rod group is built (one rod group), and the screw group is sized ceil(80/40)=2; the chain is feasible (20/min ingot <= 200 ceiling) so the new OUT port derives 80/min. Every asserted value matches the code's output, so this is a pass-by-construction probe with a correct expectation and correct code -- neither NEW_BUG (actual == expected) nor SPEC_DRIFT (expectation is right). The referenced error-context.md is absent and renderer/test-results/.last-run.json reports status "passed" with empty failedTests, i.e. it did not reproduce on the flake-filter re-run. The likely trigger is a harness timing/flake in the serialized shared-bridge batch (e.g. a transient store/derived or hydrate read during openGraph's page.goto re-sync, or the modal-hide/tidy_layout relayout momentarily detaching the port-out node), not a logic defect in MAKE reuse scale-up.
fixHint: No product change. Treat as a flake and keep the assertions as-is. To harden the probe against shared-bridge/hydrate timing: after clicking mfr-build and asserting the modal is hidden, poll hydrate until the rod group's count settles (expect.poll on countRecipe/groupsOf) before reading counts, and assert port-out-Desc_IronScrew_C only after a stable hydrate; optionally add a brief settle on openGraph so existingProducers/derived are populated before interacting.

test: undo-all / redo-all round-trips the plan hash over a scripted sequence
summary: resetView does not reset plan or undo journal, so probe 1's undo-all drains the whole shared audit journal and trips the guard-over-50 throw.
detail: Test-harness bug, not product. resetView (renderer/e2e/helpers.ts:31-34) only rewrites viewState; the real reset is /api/new_empire (crates/app/src/session.rs:1639-1653). The audit shares one accumulating plan/journal (renderer/playwright.config.ts:5-20), and the undo log is unbounded (crates/planner-core/src/undo.rs). planner-core-probes runs after import-probes etc., so canUndo is true and depth exceeds 50 when probe 1 starts; undo-all (spec lines 111-117) throws at guard over 50 on line 115, and H_empty (line 81) never equals the drained empty-plan hash on line 127. Product round-trip is sound (undo.rs:150-179, 989-1014). Fix: seed with /api/new_empire or undo exactly 10 / redo 10.
fixHint: Use POST /api/new_empire to seed probe 1 instead of resetView, or undo exactly 10 and redo 10 rather than draining to canUndo/canRedo false.

test: a good Docs.json upload preserves the existing plan through the swap and reload
summary: A web-only probe was driven against the dev-bridge harness where the __WASM_BACKEND__-gated docs upload input does not exist, so setInputFiles times out; the product code and expectation are correct.
detail: The probe (renderer/e2e-audit/web-probes.spec.ts:244-270) REQUIRES the built web app (its own header, lines 9-21: `pnpm build:web`/`dist-web` + `vite preview` + `__WASM_BACKEND__` on, i.e. playwright.web.config.ts). But it lives in e2e-audit/, which is the testDir of playwright.audit.config.ts (line 7). That config spreads `...base` from playwright.config.ts, whose webServer is the dev bridge (`cargo run … dev-bridge`, port 8791) + `pnpm dev`. vite.config.ts:21 sets `__WASM_BACKEND__` true ONLY for `mode === "web"`, so under `pnpm dev` it is false and DataMenu.tsx:400 dead-code-drops the `<input data-testid="docs-file-input">`. Probe 3's `await page.getByTestId("docs-file-input").setInputFiles(DOCS_FIXTURE)` (web-probes.spec.ts:256) therefore targets a zero-match locator → Playwright auto-waits for an actionable element and times out → FAIL. Sibling probe 1 guards this exact case with `test.skip((await page.getByTestId("docs-file-input").count()) === 0, "web-only: bridge build has no docs upload input")` (line 141); probe 3 omits the guard. The declared expectation is CORRECT and the product code is correct: wasmWorker.uploadDocs (renderer/src/state/wasmWorker.ts:236-245) copies the preserved plan blob into `new WebSession(bytes, planCopy)`; WebSession::new (crates/web/src/lib.rs:92-118) tags the catalog "uploaded" when docs are present; Session::with_store (crates/app/src/session.rs:375-430) decodes+parse_docs the fixture successfully; saveDocsAndPlan writes DOCS_KEY+KEY atomically; and on reload ensureReady step 1 (wasmWorker.ts:180-182) reconstructs (docs, blob) preserving SURVIVOR with buildVersion "uploaded". The equivalent good-upload+reload flow is already green in renderer/e2e-web/web-smoke.spec.ts Phase 4a (lines 287-352). No pass-1 finding covers this path (audit finding #1 is the empty-`[]` false-success = probe 2, a different case). Corrected expectation: under the correct web-build harness this probe passes; its failure on the bridge is a harness/guard defect, not a product bug.
fixHint: Run web-probes.spec.ts under playwright.web.config.ts (built web app, __WASM_BACKEND__ on) as its header instructs — e.g. move/copy it beside e2e-web, or point a web-build config's testDir at it. As a defensive belt-and-suspenders, add probe 1's guard `test.skip((await page.getByTestId("docs-file-input").count()) === 0, "web-only: bridge build has no docs upload input")` to probe 3 (and probe 2) so they self-skip instead of hard-failing when accidentally run on the bridge.

test: a wasm undo snapshot persists the undone state across reload
summary: The undo probe is a web-build-only IndexedDB-snapshot test that was mistakenly run against the dev-bridge build via playwright.audit.config.ts, where its mechanism doesn't apply and its per-test-isolation assumption is false; the underlying undo-persistence code is correct.
detail: web-probes.spec.ts (header lines 9-21) explicitly REQUIRES the web harness (dist-web + vite preview + __WASM_BACKEND__, wasm Session over IndexedDB) and states it CANNOT run under playwright.audit.config.ts (dev-bridge on 8791 + pnpm dev). But playwright.audit.config.ts:7 sets testDir "./e2e-audit" over the bridge base config (playwright.config.ts:22-55, baseURL localhost:5173), so this file was swept into the BRIDGE run. Probe 4 (spec line 282) uniquely runs to a spurious failure because, unlike its siblings, it never touches the __WASM_BACKEND__-gated docs-file-input: probe 1 (line 141) has test.skip on `docs-file-input count===0` and skips on the bridge; probes 2/3 setInputFiles on that absent input and fail fast; probe 4 uses only dispatch(create_factory)/undo()/reload(), all of which exist on the dev build (__ficsitStore is exposed via store.ts:1246 `import.meta.env.DEV || __WASM_BACKEND__`), so it executes against the wrong backend with no guard. On the bridge its absolute-count assertions (toBe(2) at line 290, toBe(1) at 294/300) break: the web harness gives each test a fresh browser context ⇒ empty IndexedDB (header 16-21), but the bridge is one shared server-side plan file (/tmp/ficsit-e2e-world.ficsit, wiped once per run, playwright.config.ts:12-20) and these web probes deliberately skip the resetView that the other 11 audit specs use — so factories seeded by sibling probes (e.g. probe 3's SURVIVOR, persisted via BridgeBackend POST before it fails at the missing docs input) leak in and the first count assertion sees extras. The declared mechanism ('a wasm undo snapshot persists ... across reload' over IndexedDB) also has no referent on the bridge (no wasm, no IndexedDB; durability is the server plan file). Corrected expectation: this probe should run under playwright.web.config.ts (or carry a web-build test.skip like probe 1). The product code it targets is correct: Session::undo (crates/app/src/session.rs:581-609) applies the inverse batch then store.checkpoint(batch, meta, applied_count()); applied_count()=undo.entries().len()=entries[..cursor].len()=1 after undo (crates/planner-core/src/undo.rs:58-59); MemoryPlanStore::checkpoint (crates/persist/src/memory.rs:214-231) removes BETA's row via apply_rows and sets undo_cursor=1; export_snapshot (memory.rs:319) captures entities={ALPHA}; on reload load() rebuilds state from entity rows ⇒ ALPHA only. So under the intended web harness the probe passes.
fixHint: Run web-probes.spec.ts under playwright.web.config.ts (dist-web build) rather than playwright.audit.config.ts, or add a web-build guard to probe 4 mirroring probe 1's `test.skip((await page.getByTestId("docs-file-input").count()) === 0, "web-only")` so it skips on the bridge instead of asserting against it.

test: view-state (openFactory) round-trips through IndexedDB on reload
summary: The probe's own waitReady() helper hard-codes a map-view boot assumption (requires the "map-root" test id visible), which is incompatible with the exact behavior this test asserts — after the reload the app correctly re-opens factory F, so it renders GraphView (graph-root), and map-root is intentionally absent, making waitReady time out.
detail: The product code round-trips view-state correctly; the failure is in the test harness, not the app. Trace: setView({mode:"factory",factoryId:F}) → store.ts:768-782 saveViewState → backend.setViewState({openFactory:F}). On the wasm build the worker applies set_view_state to the session store's meta immediately (crates/web/src/lib.rs:195-203 → session.rs:1620-1623 → memory.rs:233-235 set_meta("view_state",...)) and merely arms the debounce; the subsequent create_factory FLUSH calls snapshotNow() (wasmWorker.ts:281-285,319-325) which export_blob()s the whole store — meta included (memory.rs:319-321) — to IndexedDB. On reload ensureReady reconstructs from that blob (memory.rs:96-119) and hydrate re-projects: store.ts:631,646-649 sets view={mode:"factory",factoryId:F} because init.viewState.openFactory===F and plan.factories[F] exists. So the round-trip SUCCEEDS. But App.tsx:207-210 renders `<GraphView>` (not `<MapView>`) whenever view.mode==="factory", and the "map-root" data-testid lives ONLY on MapView (MapView.tsx:802-803); GraphView exposes "graph-root" instead (GraphView.tsx:824). The probe's waitReady (web-probes.spec.ts:64-78) begins with `await expect(page.getByTestId("map-root")).toBeVisible({timeout:30_000})` and is invoked post-reload at line 349 — where the view is now the factory, so map-root never appears and waitReady times out at ~30s, failing the test before line 350-351's (satisfiable) view assertion is even reached. The failure is therefore caused BY the correct behavior. Secondary harness issue: the only wired config, playwright.audit.config.ts:7, extends the dev-bridge base (playwright.config.ts) rather than the required web build (playwright.web.config.ts) — the spec header (lines 9-21) says these probes MUST run under the web harness — but this does not change the verdict: the bridge also persists view-state (dev_bridge.rs:111 /api/view + session.rs:452 hydrate viewState survive a reload since the reload does not restart the bridge process), so the reload lands in factory view under either backend and waitReady's map-root gate fails identically. The declared EXPECTATION (app reopens the factory) is right and met — this is not spec drift; it is a wrong-selector infra defect in the probe's readiness helper.
fixHint: Give this probe a factory-aware readiness check after reload: instead of waitReady() (which asserts map-root), wait for the reopened factory view — e.g. `await expect(page.getByTestId("graph-root")).toBeVisible({timeout:30_000})` plus the existing __ficsitStore ready/error poll (drop the map-root assertion) — or parameterize waitReady to accept the expected view.mode. Separately, point the suite at playwright.web.config.ts (web build, __WASM_BACKEND__) as the spec header requires, rather than playwright.audit.config.ts which extends the dev-bridge base.
